title: unable to attach to terminal for ubuntu 25.10 amd64 image when running podman in macos
body:
### Issue Description

unable to attach to terminal for ubuntu 25.10 amd64 image when running podman in macos



### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
$ podman run -it --arch amd64 ubuntu:25.10 bash
Resolved "ubuntu" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/ubuntu:25.10...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:a206cee1e8395be2f750c7fa3ff5be629dc0dea7ef665cc4c7aceaf244fcf0f8
Copying config sha256:62fa931b077876aa2304474c60c894bb5212ee526e964ba3d6737b9afae9c51d
Writing manifest to image destination
```
(stuck here)

### Describe the results you received

command stuck after starting container

### Describe the results you expected

a bash shell is opened

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: I was able to reproduce this on my main machine, but I am running macOS 15.7.2 with Rosetta disabled for Podman. Do you have Rosetta enabled on your system? Interestingly, I could not reproduce the issue on a system using Podman client v5.7.0 and a v5.5.2 machine where Rosetta is enabled.

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

I believe rosetta is enabled?
https://podman-desktop.io/docs/podman/rosetta
> You will see the rosetta configuration parameter with either true or false. If the parameter does not exist, Rosetta is already enabled by default.

```
$ cat  ~/.config/containers/containers.conf
[containers]
[engine]
env = []

[machine]
provider = "libkrun"

[network]
[secrets]
[configmaps]

```
Ah, to use Rosetta, you need to use the `applehv` provider.

Tried to recreate podman VM with applehv provider, still having same problem no matter rosetta is enabled or not.
(Yes, I remembered to recreate the VM when rosetta setting is changed.)

Is Rosetta enabled on your macOS?

```
pkgutil --pkg-info com.apple.pkg.RosettaUpdateAuto
```

Rosetta is installed.

```
$ pkgutil --pkg-info com.apple.pkg.RosettaUpdateAuto
package-id: com.apple.pkg.RosettaUpdateAuto
version: 1.0.0.0.1761698074
volume: /
location: /
install-time: 1762769625
```
Sorry, I didn't catch that sooner. Take a look at this blog post: https://blog.podman.io/2025/08/podman-5-6-released-rosetta-status-update/

Confirm the problem disappeared after rosetta is enabled. No wonder why podman is so slow when I started a amd64 linux container to compile software.

(I only switched to use mac mini recently because my old linux notebook had a failed battery, so I am not aware of such quirks.)
